title: Project Manager | IT | one-north
description:
Job Scope:

  • Support end-to-end project management for IT systems — from budget request and tender award to implementation, maintenance, and support.+
  • Drive system development efforts including user and vendor management, deployment coordination (that may include weekend support), and stakeholder engagement.
  • Manage and ensure compliance with IM8 governance and audit requirements, including resolution of VAPT findings and assessment of audit/security risks.
  • Manage and support systems during the warranty and maintenance phases to ensure smooth day-to-day operations and end-user satisfaction.
  • Provide timely incident and case management support, including troubleshooting, user support management, and coordination with relevant parties.
  • Handle full spectrum of procurement activities, including papers for approval of requirements, technical specifications, reviewing vendor proposals, and ensuring proper contract execution.
  • Handle project-related documentation for approval processes, including project updates to management.
  • Ensure systems are delivered in line with operational needs, within scope, budget, and timeline.
  • Coordinate meetings, track NOM reviews, and carry out secretariat duties including minute-taking, agenda collation, and document circulation.


Requirements

  • Tertiary qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
  • Certification in project management and/or technical domain is an advantage.
  • Strong understanding of IM8 compliance and government procurement processes.
  • Preferably over 5 years of relevant working experience in IT project and operations management.

Skills and Experience

Project Management

  • Contract management and vendor coordination
  • Stakeholder management across internal teams and external partners
  • System operations support, including infrastructure integration and service continuity
  • Writing technical documentation such as requirement specs, system design documents, test plans and reports
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to resolve incidents with minimal operational disruption.

Technical

  • Managing both on-premises and cloud systems preferred
  • Cloud technology and application modernisation
  • Containers and orchestration platforms (advantageous)
  • Process and data integration and exchange

Personal characteristics

  • Strong planning and project management abilities.
  • Able to work both independently and collaboratively as a team player.
  • Effective interpersonal and communication skills to engage diverse stakeholders.
  • Proactive, adaptable, and able to lead initiatives with minimal supervision.


Working Arrangement

  • Mon – Thurs : 8:30am – 6:00pm
  • Friday : 8:30am – 5:30pm
  • (inclusive of 1-hour lunch break)


Contract Duration: 2 years
Location: one-north
Salary: $5000 to $7000

EA License No.: 96C4864 (Shanice Lim Xin Ni)
Reg. No.: R22110485
